mission.title: kcp: APIExport consumer can't wildcard list/watch its schemas until the first APIBinding is created
mission.description: APIExport consumer can't wildcard list/watch its schemas until the first APIBinding is created. Community-reported issue.

steps.title: Identify kcp troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your kcp deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kcp -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kcp
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kcp -n kcp --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review kcp configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kcp configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kcp -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kcp
kubectl get configmap -n kcp -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kcp
```
A controller using APIExports can't successfully start up and wildcard list/watch the APIs it's exporting until the first APIBinding is created.

steps.title: Apply the fix for APIExport consumer can't wildcard list/watch its schemas…
steps.description:
there is a problem though with the vw solution: what about watches? They have to start and then eventually show objects when the first APIBinding is created on that shard. That's tricky. We could have a fake storage for identity based requests always returning empty, or a watch without events. When
```yaml
https://$server/services/apiexport/root:default:andy/controller-runtime-example-data.my.domain/clusters/*/apis/data.my.domain/v1alpha1/widgets
```

steps.title: Confirm APIExport consumer can't wildcard list/watch its… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kcp -n kcp --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n kcp --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: there is a problem though with the vw solution: what about watches? They have to start and then eventually show objects when the first APIBinding is created on that shard. That's tricky. We could have a fake storage for identity based requests always returning empty, or a watch without events.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kcp installed or the issue environment reproducible.
